name: brainstorming
tagline: Guides collaborative dialogue to turn a raw idea into an approved design spec before any implementation begins.
verdict: Clear, well-structured process skill: named output artifact and path, a self-review checklist, worked example prompts, and a hard gate against premature implementation. No security concerns. Main gaps: no stated non-goals despite a very broad trigger ('any creative work'), no guidance if git commit or the visual-companion server fails, and no.

highlights.summary: No 'NOT for' or scope-exclusion text anywhere, despite a deliberately broad trigger covering 'any creative work'.
User-rejection loop is well handled, but failure of git commit or of starting the visual-companion server is never addressed.
No guidance for re-running the skill on the same topic/day, e.g. a spec file already existing at the dated path.
